description_long: This is a vertical traffic sign mounted on a slender cylindrical post with a greenish hue and a hexagonal base. The signboard at the top is rectangular with a castle-like crenellated upper edge, and it displays the words 'HOT ALGAE' in bold white letters, with a smaller line of text below. The sign's surface appears to be made of metal or a similar rigid material, with a weathered, slightly mottled green and white finish. The post is relatively thin and uniform, suggesting it is designed for easy visibility without obstructing pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall construction is typical of urban signage, intended to be both durable and easily readable from a distance.
description: A green, post-mounted traffic sign with a crenellated top and bold white text reading 'HOT ALGAE', designed for urban visibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full signboard with 'HOT ALGAE' text and crenellated top clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the signboard and the cylindrical post, with minimal visible text.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the back of the signboard and post, with the sign's shape and mounting evident.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and post, with no readable text.
